title: neottia caurina
intro: Neottia caurina, also known as the Western rattlesnake plantain or Zig-Zag Orchid, is a perennial orchid native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its distinctive zigzagged stems and white or greenish flowers. Traditionally, Neottia caurina has been used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in the recovery from illnesses. In herbal preparations, Neottia caurina can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its effects.
